Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-0~ 
Dear Ms. Grace: 
Thank you for your request for advice on the campaign 
reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
FACTS 
You are the Treasurer for the West Hollywood Incorporation 
Committee (WEIC). 
WEIC, in an effort to incorporate West 
Hollywood, has filed an application with the Local Agency 
Formation Commission (LAFCO). 
LAFCO will hold hearings and 
approve or disapprove the application. 
If it is approved, the 
issue will go before the Boara of Supervisors which will decide 
whether to submit the matter to the voters. 
WHIC has filed its 
Statement of Organization and one campaign statement. 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
What are WHIC's campaign filing requirements? 
DISCUSSION 
Although waIC has filed one campaign stateme~t, it actually 
has no reporting responsibilities until the incorporation 
proposal becomes a "measure." 
This will occur when the Board of 
Supervisors orders the proposal placed on the ballot. 
(See the 
enclosed copy of the Fontana Opinion, No. 75-162, February 4, 
1976.) 
In the meantime, you have properly filed WHIC's 
Statement of Organization and you should maintain records of 
receipts and expenditures for WHlC's future campaign reporting 
responsibilities. 


Cyn thi a Grace 
March 21, 1984 
Page 2 
Once the incorporation proposal becomes a "measure," wrIIC 
will be a committee primarily formed to support the measure. 
WHIC's filing deadline will depend upon the date of the 
election. 
(See the "1984 Information Manual on the Campaign 
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act," pgs. 6-9, 
Sections B-D.) 
The first campaign statement filed will cover 
tne period beginning with the day after the closing date of the 
last statement filed by your committee. 
It will include all 
contributions received, and expenditures made, for the purpose 
of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the 
voters for the passage of the measure, even if the contributions 
and expenditures occurred prior to the proposal becoming a 
"measure." 
(See the enclosed copies of 2 Cal. Adm. Code 
Sections 18215 and 18225.) 
You will not be required to report 
any receipts or payments that were made for the purpose of 
influencing the LAFCO or the Board of Supervisors. 
If the Board of Supervisors disapproves the request for 
incorporation and WHIC decides to attempt to qualify the 
proposal as an initiative, please contact our office again, as 
w~IC will incur additional reporting responsibilities. 
If you have any additional questions, please feel free to 
contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosure 
Very truly yours,

Counsel 
Legal Division

Secretary of State 
1230 J St. 
Political Reform Division 
Sacramento. Calif •• 
Dear Secretary of State. 
... 
l 
MAR 7 12 42 PH "tl4 
March 1. 1984 
ID # 84025J 
I am writin~ on behalf of the West Hollywood Incorporation 
Committee. of which I am Treasurer. to request clarification with 
respect to the filing dates for campaign statements. 
Cur state-
ment of organization (form 410) was filed on Feb. 2. 1984. 
Our 
first campai~n statement (form 420) was filed on Feb. 9: it 
covered the period from the first reportable transaction to the 
time of qualification. 
The 1983 Information Manual. published by the FPPC, states. 
at page 9. Section E, that committees formed to qualify a measure 
for the ballotmust file a campaign statement either 35 days after 
the deadline for filing petitions, or 35 days after the notice 
of qualification. 
WHIC is seeking to incorporate West Hollywood under procedures 
set forth in the Municipal Organization Act. OUr application to the 
Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO) includes a petition. 
(Govt. Code 35100) 
However. the petition alone. even if sufficient, 
does not automatically qualify the measure for the ballot. 
There is no 
deadline for the submission of the petition. 
either in sate statute. or in the administrative procedures of 
the local agency. 
Further~ore. we are not. by virtue of the 
application, automatically guaranteed an election date. 
Lengthy hearings will be held before LAFCO and the Board of 
Supervisors. (Attached for your information is a synopsis of the 
statutory time frame for holding these proceedings.) 
LAFCO has 
broad powers with respect to whether to approve or deny the 
application. 
The Board of Supervisors has discretion as to 
whether to submit the question to the voters for confirmation. 
An election date. if any, will be determined by the sequence of 
hearin~ dates before LAFCO and the Board of Superv~sors. There 
is no "notice of qualification.n 
However, the order of confirmation 
by the Board of Supervisors (Govt. Code 35257) may be roughly 
analogous. 
Since WHIC does not quite fall into the five categories of 
committees described in your manual. please advise me when I 
should file future campaign statements. 
Yours truly. 
F'I

Cynthia Grace 
Attorney at Law

making its determination.

posal involving any of the same territory may be filed with 
LAFCO for at least one year from date of disapproval without 
the consent of the commission. G.C. 54799)

less than 50% voter

~ Tues. after 1st Mon. 
November each year.

E.C. 17088

results of the election 
& submit it to the Board 
within 28 days of the 
election. 
After canvass of election 
G.C. 35261-63 
returns, Board must adopt 
resolution either termi-
nating proceedings (if 
majority vote against) or 
confirming its order of 
incorporation (if majority 
favor). 
If election successful, 
G.C. 35354 
Board sends LAFCO certified 
copy of final resolution; 
LAFCO executes and records 
Certificate of completion. 
Incorportion becomes effective 
on date of recordation. 
(If election fails, no new proposal of the same or substan-
tially the same territory may be filed with LAFCO for at 
least two years from date of resolution terminating pro-
ceedings, unless such provisions are waived by LAFCO. G.C. 35264) 
*No time specified in Code.
